items.mistake: Drawing Gen AI as a fourth rung strictly above Deep Learning
items.fix: Show Gen AI as a generative branch under ML, usually implemented with deep nets—not a layer that replaces DL.
items.why: Most Gen AI systems *are* deep learning systems; the distinction is task (generate vs predict), not a separate hardware stack.
items.example: An LLM is deep learning + autoregressive generation. Saying "we moved from DL to Gen AI" obscures that the model is still a deep neural network.

items.mistake: Calling every AI system "Generative AI" because it uses a neural network
items.fix: Ask what the deployed output is: a label/score (discriminative) or newly sampled content (generative).
items.why: ResNet image classification and BERT sentiment scoring are deep ML but not Gen AI products unless they synthesize outputs.
items.example: A fraud model outputting `p_fraud=0.02` is ML/AI; a chatbot drafting an email is Gen AI.

items.mistake: Duplicating LLM fundamentals on a taxonomy page
items.fix: Keep placement and vocabulary here; link to what-is-genai and llms-fundamentals for mechanisms, scaling, and training stages.
items.why: Fundamentals belong in the Gen AI Fundamentals stage; this classical-ML framing topic should stay navigational and conceptual.
items.example: Instead of explaining BPE tokenization here, link to llms-fundamentals and generative-model-types for autoregressive details.
